title: C Arrays of Structures
content:
### Arrays of Structures in C

You can create arrays of structures to store multiple records of the same type.

### Declaration

```c
struct Student students[100];
```

### Initialization

```c
struct Student students[3] = {
  {"Alice", 20, 3.8},
  {"Bob", 22, 3.5},
  {"Charlie", 19, 3.9}
};
```

### Accessing Elements

```c
students[0].name   // first student's name
students[1].age    // second student's age
```

### Common Operations

| Operation | Code |
|-----------|------|
| Loop through | `for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)` |
| Find max | Compare member values |
| Sort | Sort by member using sorting algorithm |
| Search | Linear/binary search on a member |

### Passing to Functions

Pass the array with its size:

```c
void printStudents(struct Student arr[], int size);
```

---

> **Key Point:** Arrays of structures are useful for databases, student records, inventory lists, and any collection of related data records.

quiz.question: How do you access the name of the first student in an array of structs?
